acceptanceSignals: Admin review/document lifecycle + plant mapping works.

lastCommit.message: feat: implement Committee Admin Portal MVP (attempt 9)

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: Committee Admin Portal MVP in 0 attempts

reinforcementResults.name: Contract Lockstep Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Backend/API contract changes in this feature are synchronized with OpenAPI and generated TS client artifacts in the workspace. Contract-verification CI pass status is a merge-time gate outside this workspace snapshot, so this policy check is deferred at this stage.

reinforcementResults.name: Authorization Boundary Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: pass based on static review. Auth boundaries are centralized and enforced across UI and API: Svelte hooks + role guards isolate employee/vendor/admin routes, session expiry/refresh and rejection paths are covered in web auth tests, and privileged admin/payroll/anomaly APIs enforce bearer role plus runtime plant scope with negative tests for invalid role/scope claims. ISS-003 and ISS-007 sign-off gates are explicitly enforced before settlement/anomaly close operations (and reflected in contract schema). Runtime execution of tests was not possible in this sandbox (EPERM on tsx IPC and Cargo lock file), so verdict is based on code and test coverage inspection.

reinforcementResults.name: Canonical File Storage Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Feature scope and the active binary-artifact policy are satisfied in the current workspace. Admin compliance lifecycle captures review/suspension reasons and expiry handling; plant mapping is implemented; settlement lock/unlock/exception/dispute workflows are present with required reason capture; and anomaly/audit/analytics views support the required filters. Release sign-off gates are enforced with required issue IDs (`ISS-003` before settlement release close, `ISS-007` before anomaly release close). Binary file handling uses validated object-storage upload-plan flow with MIME/size enforcement, no runtime DB base64 artifact persistence path was found, and bucket provisioning scripts enforce private access boundaries.

reinforcementResults.name: Cache and Event Correctness Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: No concrete blockers found for the Committee Admin Portal MVP in this workspace. The implementation covers admin review/document lifecycle with suspension/expiry handling and plant mapping, settlement close/lock/unlock/exception/dispute flows, and anomaly/audit/analytics views with the required filters. `ISS-003` and `ISS-007` sign-off gating is enforced in runtime handlers and represented in the OpenAPI contract (`issueChecklist` required with strict format/uniqueness). Active policy evidence is present: cache write-through and SQL-authoritative fallback/bypass paths, idempotency coverage under replay/retry (event consumer and settlement/analytics replay tests), and DLQ routing plus alerting rules after retry thresholds. Residual risk: this is a static review; I did not execute the test suite in this read-only environment.

reinforcementResults.name: UX Reliability Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Pass. The current workspace implements the Committee Admin Portal MVP scope with required governance constraints: admin review/document lifecycle and plant mapping flows, settlement close/lock/unlock/dispute/exception flows, and anomaly/audit/analytics filtered views. ISS-003 (settlement) and ISS-007 (anomaly close) sign-off gates are enforced in both web flow logic and backend handlers. UX reliability policy checks are satisfied in code: async operations use in-flight guards plus loading states to suppress duplicate submits, API/offline failures are surfaced with zh-TW user-facing copy, and responsive role-mode behavior for employee mobile-first and vendor/admin desktop-tablet is defined and covered by platform tests.

reinforcementResults.name: No-Legacy/Fallback Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Policy checks and acceptance signals are satisfied for Committee Admin Portal MVP in this workspace: admin review/document lifecycle captures review comments, suspension reasons, and expiry-driven lifecycle transitions; vendor-plant mapping CRUD/audit is present; settlement close/lock/unlock/exception/dispute workflows are implemented with ISS-003 enforced before settlement release sign-off; anomaly CLOSE enforces ISS-007 with required closure note/evidence; anomaly/audit/analytics views expose the required filters; and the production runtime path is SQL-authoritative with in-memory-only persistence variants test-gated.

excludedApproaches: Settlement export without fixed v1 contract.
